name: Zeus Live Avatar
description: OBS scene collection for Zeus Live Avatar. Import via OBS > Scene Collection > Import.

scenes.name: ZeusAvatar
scenes.description: Full-screen Zeus MetaHuman avatar — primary scene for live calls

sources.name: Zeus UE Window
sources.description: Captures the Unreal Engine window showing the MetaHuman avatar
sources.notes: Alternative: use 'ndi_source' type if NDI output is enabled in UE

sources.name: Zeus TTS Audio
sources.description: Captures TTS audio output routed through virtual audio device
sources.notes: Route TTS audio to the virtual audio device. See SETUP.md for configuration.

filters.name: Chroma Key
filters.description: Removes green screen background from UE render (optional)
filters.notes: Enable this if using a green/blue background in Unreal Engine instead of a virtual environment

scenes.name: ScreenShare
scenes.description: Screen share with small Zeus avatar overlay in bottom-right corner

sources.name: Screen Capture
sources.description: Full screen capture of the primary monitor

sources.name: Zeus UE Window
sources.description: Small avatar overlay — same source as ZeusAvatar scene
sources.notes: Positioned bottom-right as a picture-in-picture overlay

scenes.name: SplitView
scenes.description: Side-by-side: Zeus avatar on left, screen share on right

sources.name: Zeus UE Window
sources.description: Zeus avatar on left half of screen

zeus_mic.routing: Virtual audio device → OBS Audio Capture → OBS Virtual Mic output
zeus_mic.notes: In Zoom/Meet, select 'OBS Virtual Camera' for video and the virtual audio device for microphone

virtual_camera.description: OBS Virtual Camera — select this in Zoom, Google Meet, or Teams as your webcam

import_instructions: 4. After import, configure the 'Zeus UE Window' source to point to your Unreal Engine window
5. Configure the 'Zeus TTS Audio' source to use your virtual audio device
6. If using NDI: replace 'window_capture' sources with 'ndi_source' type
7. If using green screen: enable the Chroma Key filter on ZeusAvatar scene
8. Start Virtual Camera (Tools > Start Virtual Camera)
9. In your video call app, select 'OBS Virtual Camera' as your camera
